recipes.name: Vanilla Cold Foam Iced Coffee
recipes.lede: Smooth cold brew topped with a light, velvety cold foam.
method: Fill a cup with ice and add cold brew and syrup.
Froth cold foam ingredients until thick and silky.
Gently layer foam over the coffee.
Finish with a light dusting of coffee powder.

recipes.name: Hot Vienna Coffee with Cold Foam
recipes.lede: A rich hot chocolate coffee finished with a light, velvety cold foam.
method: Heat milk to 60 to 65 °C.
Stir in chocolate syrup until fully dissolved.
Add espresso and mix gently.
Pour into a cup or heat-resistant glass.
Froth cold foam ingredients until thick and silky (pourable, not stiff).
Gently layer the cold foam over the hot drink.
Finish with cocoa powder or chocolate shavings.

recipes.name: Matcha Milk Cap Latte
recipes.lede: A vibrant iced matcha finished with a smooth, lightly salted milk cap.
method: Whisk matcha with hot water until smooth.
Add milk and sweetener, then pour over ice.
Lightly froth milk cap until thick but pourable.
Layer on top and finish with a dusting of matcha.

recipes.name: Snowcap Strawberries & Cream
recipes.lede: Fresh strawberry and milk layered with a smooth, aerated snow-cap cream topping.
method: Drizzle strawberry purée along the inside of the cup.
Add ice and milk.
Charge cream in a whipper (N₂O) and shake well.
Dispense a smooth, even layer of cream on top.
Finish with strawberry drizzle and fresh fruit garnish.

recipes.name: Caramel Nut Frappe
recipes.lede: Smooth caramel milk with a light, aerated cream topping and nutty crunch.
method: Combine milk and caramel syrup, then pour over ice.
Charge cream in a whipper (N₂O) and shake well.
Dispense a smooth snow-cap layer on top.
Finish with caramel drizzle and crushed nuts.

recipes.name: Vanilla Cream Cake Icing
recipes.lede: Classic vanilla whipped cream, light and smooth for cake icing.
method: Infuse cream: Split 5 g vanilla pod and scrape seeds into 400 g AndFoods Whip. Add 12 g sugar and combine.
Whip cream: Whip to firm peaks, stiff enough to hold shape but still smooth for spreading.
Ice cake: Use immediately to ice cake.
recipes.notes: For best results, chill the bowl and whisk before whipping. Ice the cake immediately after whipping.

recipes.name: Citrus Curd Cheesecake
recipes.lede: A composed citrus dessert: silky lime cheesecake base, airy bergamot mousse, glossy bergamot ganache, caramel mirror glaze, and bright lemon pearls.

method.heading: Lime Cheesecake
steps: Make lime mixture: Combine 50 g lime juice, 10 g corn starch, 65 g sugar, 65 g butter (melted), and 4 whole eggs and mix until smooth. Set aside.
Make cream cheese base: Heat 210 g cream cheese and 50 g milk together, stirring until smooth and lump-free. Cool, then stir in 50 g yogurt until smooth.
Combine and blend: Fold cooled lime mixture into cream cheese base. Blend with an immersion blender until fully smooth.
Add cream: Stir 115 g AndFoods Whip into the batter until smooth and fully incorporated.
Bake and set: Pour into tin. Bake in a water bath at 110 °C top and bottom heat for 90 minutes. Cool completely, then transfer to freezer.

method.heading: Bergamot Orange Mousse
steps: Bloom gelatin: Bloom 67.5 g gelatin in cold water until softened.
Mix yolks and sugar: Combine 375 g yolk and 240 g sugar together.
Temper yolks: Heat 600 g milk and 200 g bergamot fruit puree together until just simmering. Slowly pour over yolk mixture, whisking constantly to temper.
Cook custard base: Return mixture to saucepan. Cook over medium heat, stirring continuously, until thickened enough to coat the back of a spoon.
Add gelatin: Remove from heat. Squeeze excess water from gelatin and stir into hot custard until fully dissolved. Cool to room temperature.
Whip cream: Whip 2100 g AndFoods Whip to soft peaks.
Fold together: Fold whipped cream into cooled custard base in two additions until smooth and uniform.
Set: Pour into moulds or set vessel. Refrigerate until fully set.

method.heading: Bergamot Orange Ganache
steps: Bloom gelatin: Bloom 5 g gelatin in cold water until softened.
Heat cream base: Heat 220 g AndFoods Whip, 80 g bergamot fruit puree, 8 g glucose, and 8 g inverted sugar together until just simmering.
Melt chocolate: Pour hot cream over 140 g white chocolate and let sit 1 minute, then stir until fully melted and smooth.
Add gelatin: Squeeze excess water from gelatin and stir into hot ganache until dissolved.
Set: Cool to room temperature, then refrigerate until set before use.

method.heading: Caramel Mirror Glaze
steps: Bloom gelatin: Bloom 15 g gelatin in cold water until softened.
Make caramel: Cook 375 g sugar and 300 g water in a saucepan over medium-high heat without stirring until deep amber caramel forms.
Deglaze with cream: Carefully pour in 300 g AndFoods Whip and stir until smooth. Return to heat if needed to dissolve any lumps.
Add starch slurry: Whisk 25 g corn starch into 50 g water until smooth. Stir into the hot caramel and cook briefly until thickened.
Add gelatin: Remove from heat. Squeeze excess water from gelatin and stir in until fully dissolved.
Cool and use: Strain through a fine sieve. Cool to 35 to 40 °C before glazing.

method.heading: Lemon Decoration Gel
steps: Bloom gelatin: Bloom 5 g gelatin in cold water until softened.
Add NH pectin: Heat 150 g lemon juice until warm. Whisk in 8 g NH pectin powder and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add gelatin: Remove from heat. Squeeze excess water from gelatin and stir in until fully dissolved.
Pipe pearls: While still warm, pipe small dots onto the cake to form pearl decorations. Refrigerate until set.

recipes.notes: Do not whip the AndFoods Whip in the cheesecake base, it is added as a liquid. Pour the mirror glaze at 35 to 40 °C for best coverage. Pipe the lemon pearls while the gel is still warm.

recipes.name: Cream Profiterole
recipes.lede: Choux-style profiteroles with layered cream fillings: custard, crème diplomat, and pistachio.

method.heading: Custard Filling
steps: Heat cream base: Heat 3500 g AndFoods Whip, 800 g sugar, and 40 g vanilla sauce over medium until the mixture reaches 70 °C and sugar dissolves.
Mix egg and starch: Whisk 800 g egg pulp and 200 g corn starch together until smooth and lump-free.
Combine: Pour hot cream into egg mixture, whisking constantly to combine.
Cook custard: Return mixture to saucepan. Stir continuously over medium heat until thickened and bubbling. Cook out for a further 1 to 2 minutes.
Add butter: Off heat, stir in 500 g vegan butter in chunks until fully melted and glossy.
Cool and set: Transfer to a tray, press cling film directly on surface, and refrigerate until set.

method.heading: Crème Diplomat
steps: Whip cream: Whip 1000 g AndFoods Whip to stiff peaks.
Prep custard: Ensure 1000 g custard sauce is fully chilled and smooth. Whisk briefly to loosen if needed.
Fold together: Fold whipped cream into custard in two additions until just combined. Do not overmix.
Use or chill: Use immediately or refrigerate and pipe within the same day.

method.heading: Pistachio Cream Filling
steps: Whip cream: Whip 100 g AndFoods Whip and 10 g sugar together to stiff peaks.
Add pistachio paste: Fold in 30 g pistachio paste until evenly combined.
Use or chill: Use immediately or refrigerate until ready to pipe.

recipes.notes: Strain custard for smoothness. Fold whipped cream gently into the diplomat to keep it light. Pipe each filling chilled.

recipes.name: Chocolate Ganache Filling
recipes.lede: Rich chocolate ganache filling, smooth, glossy, and pipeable.
method: Heat cream base: Heat 250 g AndFoods Whip, 50 g milk, and 30 g cocoa powder together, whisking until smooth and just simmering.
Melt chocolate: Pour hot cream over 25 g chocolate. Let sit 1 minute, then stir until fully melted and glossy.
Cool and set: Cool to room temperature, then refrigerate until set and pipeable.
recipes.notes: Ensure the ganache is fully cooled before piping. Use an immersion blender for a smoother finish if needed.
